title: Fluentd Worker Process Crash and Supervisor Restart Detection
spl:
index=fluent sourcetype="fluentd:log"
| search "worker" AND ("died" OR "exit" OR "SIGSEGV" OR "respawn" OR "unexpected error")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count, values(worker_id) as workers by _time, host
| where count >= 3
| sort -count
description: Fluentd multi-worker mode amplifies throughput but multiplies crash blast radius; rapid respawns indicate plugin native crashes, OOM kills, or poison messages. Early detection preserves at-least-once delivery assumptions.
value: Reduces unexplained log holes during deployments and flags unstable custom plugins.
implementation: Include Kubernetes `pod_name` for DaemonSets. Join to node memory pressure metrics. Page when `count` exceeds baseline during non-deploy windows.
visualization: Timeline annotations, bar chart (restarts by host), table with linked stack traces.
grandmaExplanation: We count how often worker processes die and come back. Frequent restarts mean the log shipper is unstable and you could lose messages between crashes.
